name: Create an export capability for Atlassian's Jira so that it can import for SOC2

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented Goal `00000SZ1RWPTTJX3GJ6HDS2000`; the worktree is ready for review.

After-action report:

- Added a shared [Jira evidence export service](/home/buddy/projects/refine-next/.git/refine-worktrees/refine-00000SZ1RWPTTJX3GJ6HDS2000-round-1/src/tools/product/goal_exports/mod.rs:1) producing Jira-importable CSV with Goal metadata, prompts, implementation reports, quality/governance results, notes, and exact commit history.
- Exposed it through `refine goal export <ID> [--output FILE]`, `GET /work/goals/{id}/export/jira`, the Goal UI’s “Export for Jira” action, and MCP’s `refine_export_goal_jira`.
- Centralized formatting so every surface returns identical evidence. The CSV follows Atlassian’s required `Summary` field and multiline quoting rules. [Atlassian CSV import documentation](https://support.atlassian.com/jira-cloud-administration/docs/import-data-from-a-csv-file/)

Verification:

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `cargo test --quiet -- --unit` — passed, 358 tests.
- Focused export tests — passed, 4/4 under both filters.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — passed.
- `cargo test --doc` — passed, 0 failures.
- `cargo build --release --locked` — passed.
- Static asset check — passed, 53 files.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Daemon CLI suite — the new export step passed, but the suite later failed twice at existing cluster-sync assertion line 852 because it received `Waiting for the repository worker` instead of a completed payload.

Goal-agent usage: 287,199 tokens over approximately 16 minutes 31 seconds.
rounds.prompt: Create an export capability for Atlassian's Jira so that it can import for SOC2 compliance that includes what was done, commits, etc. This follow other functionality around after implementation reports. All surfaces should have this capability. Decide where is best to put this in the web UI.
